title: cephalanthera pallens subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Cephalanthera pallens subsp. ochroleuca, also known as the pale form of the white helleborine, is a species of orchid native to Europe. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its chemical compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Cephalanthera pallens subsp. ochroleuca include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
